management_notes:
CEO Shaun Heinrichs brings 25+ years of mining finance and operations experience, including prior CFO roles at Veris Gold and VMS Ventures. VP Exploration Michele Della Libera (appointed April 2024) is a credentialed discovery geologist credited with defining the 10+ Moz Blackwater deposit at New Gold. COO Éric Vinet was appointed December 2025 and received 800,000 options plus 300,000 RSUs on appointment — a signal of board conviction in the mine-development transition. Strategic anchor Eric Sprott holds approximately 17.2% of shares and participated in the July 2025 C$13.2M bought-deal financing, increasing his stake from ~16.7%.

SEDI disclosures and press releases confirm no insider selling. The US$30M Auramet credit facility (February 2026) was secured on management's track record and project quality — an external validation from a gold trading and streaming firm. The team composition covers geology, operations, and capital markets across the key disciplines required for a mine restart.

One minor governance concern: CEO Heinrichs concurrently serves as CFO of NiCAN Limited, creating a secondary time commitment during the most execution-intensive phase of the company's history. This should be monitored as the True North mine restart accelerates through 2026-2027.
geology_notes:
True North Gold Project (Manitoba) hosts a NI 43-101 compliant resource (effective August 2024): 499,000 oz indicated at 4.41 g/t Au and 644,000 oz inferred at 3.65 g/t Au. The adjacent Ogama-Rockland deposit adds 337,000 oz inferred at an exceptional 8.17 g/t Au, yielding a combined portfolio of approximately 1.48 Moz across all categories. A February 2026 PEA confirms robust economics: after-tax NPV(5%) of US$391M at US$3,000/oz gold, 105% IRR, and a low initial capex of C$59.2M — one of the most capital-efficient gold development studies released in Canada in recent years. At US$4,800/oz gold, the PEA NPV reaches US$998M.

The deposit style is high-grade Archean orogenic veins in the Rice Lake greenstone belt. True North is a past-producing mine (fully permitted) with all critical infrastructure in place, including a processing facility and tailings management area. Underground infill drilling (2 rigs since November 2025) is confirming resource continuity, with recent results including 10.79 g/t Au over 2.20 m (Hinge Zone) and 62.40 g/t Au over 1.00 m (San Antonio West). A 2,200-metre surface drill program at Ogama-Rockland commenced December 2025, with an updated MRE expected H1 2026.

The primary geological risk is inherent to narrow-vein underground mining: grade and width variability can impact diluted production grades and mining costs. The PEA's 58,100 oz/yr steady-state production assumes tight drill density and selective mining — execution discipline will determine whether headline resource grades translate to mill feed grades.
capital_notes:
The company has approximately 262 million basic shares outstanding (296 million fully diluted as of September 2025), a market cap of approximately C$225-265M, and approximately C$26M in cash. A C$23M financing closed December 2025 at C$1.43/share; a US$30M credit facility with Auramet International was secured in February 2026, with US$15M drawn in Tranche 1 by March 2026. The TSXV denied a separate C$20M offering announced in September 2025, creating temporary financing uncertainty before the C$23M deal was completed.

The Auramet credit facility carries a 12% annual interest rate, which is expensive relative to corporate bonds or stream financing. The facility likely includes a gold offtake component typical of trading-firm credit structures — the full terms have not been publicly disclosed. This creates uncertainty about how much of the production upside will flow to equity holders. With US$15M drawn, US$15M remaining in Tranche 2 (available Q3 2026), and C$59.2M in mine restart capex ahead, additional financing will be required.

The capital structure is functional but stressed. The expensive debt, undisclosed offtake terms, and need for further financing before production make this the weakest factor in the scorecard.
catalyst_notes:
The February 2026 PEA release was the defining near-term catalyst, confirming a low-capex, high-return mine restart case. An updated NI 43-101 resource for Ogama-Rockland (currently 337,000 oz inferred at 8.17 g/t Au) is expected H1 2026 — if the resource grows substantially (400,000+ oz at grade), it could meaningfully increase the combined resource base and strengthen the economics of a future expansion beyond True North.

Active underground infill and delineation drilling at True North (2 rigs) is generating steady news flow. San Antonio West surface drilling has returned grades up to 62.40 g/t Au over 1.00 m, confirming the district's high-grade exploration upside. The mine restart milestones for 2026 include: crushing circuit installation, underground development, and Tranche 2 credit drawdown (Q3 2026). First production is targeted for 2027.

The catalyst calendar through 2026 is well-populated and near-term. The Ogama-Rockland MRE is the highest-impact single event on the horizon.
acquisition_notes:
1911 Gold trades at approximately 0.46x its PEA after-tax NPV(5%) of US$391M at US$3,000/oz gold, implying the enterprise value (approximately USD$180M) offers a material discount to the base-case economic value. At current gold prices (~US$3,200/oz), the NPV would exceed the base-case modelling by a meaningful margin. EV per in-situ ounce is approximately US$122/oz across all 1.48 Moz (all categories), which is consistent with comparable high-grade underground gold development-stage peers at US$100-300/oz.

The low initial capex of C$59.2M is the strongest acquisition value argument: an acquirer with mine-building capability could restart this fully permitted, past-producing mine at a fraction of what comparable underground gold mines cost to develop. The adjacent Ogama-Rockland deposit (8.17 g/t Au inferred) provides an organic growth path that would add value to any acquirer.

The undisclosed offtake terms in the Auramet facility are the primary acquisition value uncertainty. If the offtake claims a material share of gold revenue, the equity IRR available to a potential acquirer is lower than the PEA implies.
analyst_summary:
WATCH (19/25). 1911 Gold delivers one of the most capital-efficient development economics in the Canadian gold pipeline — the True North PEA (US$391M NPV at $3,000/oz, 105% IRR, C$59.2M initial capex) is genuinely compelling at current gold prices. The two strongest factors are geology (high-grade, fully permitted, past-producing mine) and catalyst proximity (Ogama-Rockland MRE H1 2026, active underground drilling confirming continuity). Eric Sprott's 17.2% anchor stake and a credible management team with mine-building experience round out a solid investment case.

The primary concerns are the expensive 12% Auramet debt facility and the undisclosed offtake terms that could cap equity upside. The capital structure will require further financing before the C$59.2M mine restart capex is fully funded — additional dilution is likely. Low direct insider ownership beyond Sprott and the CEO's concurrent NiCAN role are secondary flags. The TSXV denial of the September 2025 offering was a minor setback that adds a regulatory caution note.

The key catalyst to watch is the updated Ogama-Rockland MRE expected H1 2026. A resource materially above 400,000 oz inferred at grade would increase the combined asset base and could support a higher composite score or a BUY verdict. Full disclosure of the Auramet offtake terms would also be a key information point. Monitor the mine restart execution closely as infrastructure work commences in 2026.
